_comment: Shared prey lookup. Used by Hunter events that yield food. The Hunter finds one of these when a hunting or foraging roll succeeds. Filtered by region — the same principle as the predators file — so a Hunter in the Shire finds rabbits and pigeons, not wild hogs. Stat blocks are not reproduced; a prey creature rarely needs combat statistics.

prey.name: Deer
prey.description: A red or roe deer — substantial meat, hard to catch, a real prize for the hunter who can stalk quietly. One deer feeds the fellowship well for several days.
prey.stat_reference: No combat stats required; resolve as a Hunting roll

prey.name: Rabbits
prey.description: Rabbits or hares — plentiful, taken easily with snare or bow. Small meat but honest food, and every hunter knows their ways.

prey.name: Fowl
prey.description: Pigeon, pheasant, grouse, or wild duck depending on the country — taken by bow or net. Light meat, but quick to cook over a travelling fire.

prey.name: Fish
prey.description: Trout, perch, pike, or salmon depending on the water — patient work, but reliable where the streams are clean and the hunter has line and hook.

prey.name: Wild goats
prey.description: Mountain goats or wild kin of the hill-breeds — tough meat, hard to stalk on the broken ground they favour, but a real catch for the high-country hunter.

prey.name: Wild hogs
prey.description: A wild hog or boar, taken clean — substantial meat, dangerous to hunt, a test of skill and nerve. The wrong kill can become a gored hunter.
prey.stat_reference: A cornered boar may require combat stats — see wild_boar in predators.json
